title: dianthus subaphyllus
intro: Dianthus subaphyllus, also known as the small-leaved pink or pink-fringed pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dianthus subaphyllus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Dianthus subaphyllus can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
